title: Bomb Calorimeter Combustion Enthalpy Measurement System
description: Automates data acquisition for a bench-top bomb calorimeter experiment. A PT100/MAX31865 probe monitors water-jacket temperature at 1 Hz before and after ignition. An Arduino triggers the capacitor-discharge Nichrome ignition wire, then logs the full thermal trajectory to SD card and OLED. After the peak temperature is detected and the post-combustion drift is confirmed, the Arduino computes ΔH_comb using the bomb calorimeter energy balance and reports the result in kJ/mol.
objective: Automate ignition trigger, thermal logging, peak detection, and ΔH_comb calculation for solid fuel pellets in a bomb calorimeter; target accuracy ±0.5 % vs. benzoic acid reference (−26.434 kJ/g).
constraints: Arduino Uno, PT100/MAX31865, capacitor discharge igniter, OLED, SD logger; oxygen pressure ≤ 30 bar; only approved solid fuels (benzoic acid, sucrose, naphthalene); operator must be trained; never open bomb until T returns to baseline.
supporting_fact_titles: Combustion Enthalpy and Standard Heat of Formation

element_usage_notes.PT100 RTD Precision Temperature Module: Immersed in water jacket; reads at 1 Hz for full pre/post-combustion thermal trajectory with 0.03 °C resolution.
element_usage_notes.High-Pressure Bomb Calorimeter Vessel: Houses fuel pellet and ignition wire in pressurised O₂; submerged in calibrated water jacket.
element_usage_notes.Nichrome Ignition Wire Controller: Capacitor discharge fires ignition wire on Arduino command; single-shot, optically isolated.
element_usage_notes.SD Card Data Logger Module: Logs t, T, phase (baseline/combustion/cooling) and final ΔH_comb result.
element_usage_notes.OLED Status Display: Shows live T, elapsed time, phase status, and final ΔH_comb result post-calculation.

element_interactions.description: Capacitor discharge fires wire, igniting fuel pellet inside sealed vessel
Combustion heat raises water jacket temperature measured by PT100
Full T(t) trajectory logged; peak detection and drift correction applied post-hoc
Live temperature and computed ΔH_comb displayed on OLED
